questions.question: Which of the following best explains why political parties are necessary in modern democracies?

options.B: They organize public opinion and form governments

questions.explanation: Political parties organize public opinion, contest elections, and form governments.

questions.question: In which political system is competition between political parties absent?
questions.explanation: In a one-party system only one political party is allowed to rule.

questions.question: Which of the following situations is most likely to lead to a coalition government?

options.B: No party gets a clear majority

questions.explanation: Coalition governments form when no party gains a majority.

questions.question: Which feature distinguishes national parties from state parties in India?
questions.explanation: National parties operate across several states and influence national politics.

questions.question: Which challenge to political parties refers to concentration of power in a few leaders?
questions.explanation: Lack of internal democracy occurs when decisions are taken by a few leaders.

questions.question: Which challenge to political parties occurs when leadership passes within families?
questions.explanation: Dynastic succession refers to leadership inherited within families.

questions.question: Why might voters sometimes lack meaningful choices in elections?
questions.explanation: When major parties have similar policies, voters may feel there is little real choice.

questions.question: Which of the following best illustrates the role of opposition parties?

options.B: Criticizing government policies and suggesting alternatives

questions.explanation: Opposition parties ensure accountability by criticizing government policies.

questions.question: Which reform requires candidates to declare their criminal records and assets?
questions.explanation: Transparency reforms require candidates to disclose their background details.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the anti-defection law?

options.B: Prevent elected representatives from changing parties

questions.explanation: The anti-defection law prevents elected representatives from switching parties after election.

questions.question: Which factor allows regional parties to influence national politics?
questions.explanation: Regional parties influence national politics through coalition governments.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of political parties in law-making?
questions.explanation: Legislators often follow party policies while making laws.

questions.question: Why do political parties often form pre-election alliances?

options.B: To increase chances of winning elections

questions.explanation: Alliances strengthen electoral prospects.

questions.question: Which institution supervises recognition of national and state parties in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission recognizes political parties.

questions.question: Which challenge relates to influence of wealthy candidates in elections?
questions.explanation: Money power influences candidate selection and elections.

questions.question: Which democratic principle ensures peaceful transfer of power through elections?
questions.explanation: Democratic stability allows governments to change peacefully through elections.

questions.question: Which role of political parties helps citizens communicate with the government?
questions.explanation: Political parties help citizens communicate with government officials.

questions.question: Why are political reforms necessary for political parties?
questions.explanation: Reforms help improve transparency, democracy, and accountability.

questions.question: Which feature of political parties helps represent different social groups?
questions.explanation: Different party ideologies represent diverse social groups.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the function of political parties in policy-making?

options.A: They implement policies based on ideology

questions.explanation: Political parties implement policies according to their ideology.

questions.question: Which factor helps political parties influence public opinion?
questions.explanation: Political parties raise issues and conduct campaigns to shape public opinion.

questions.question: Which institution plays a key role in monitoring elections and political parties?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission monitors elections and political parties.

questions.question: Which of the following strengthens democratic accountability?
questions.explanation: Opposition parties ensure accountability in democracy.

questions.question: Which factor allows political parties to represent regional interests?
questions.explanation: State parties represent regional interests.

questions.question: Which political development allows smaller parties to influence government policies?
questions.explanation: Coalition politics allows smaller parties to influence government policies.
